question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>y</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></math> be the solution of the differential equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mi>d</mi><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced><mrow><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mi>cos</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></math> with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mfenced><mi>π</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mfenced></math> is equal to:
solution:
We have, xdy=y+x3cosxdx

⇒xdy=ydx+x3cosxdx

⇒xdy-ydxx2=x3cosxdxx2

⇒ddxyx=∫xcosxdx

⇒yx=xsinx-∫1.sinxdx

Therefore, yx=xsinx+cosx+C

At x=π, y=0,

0=-1+C

⇒C=1,x=π,y=0

So, yx=xsinx+cosx+1

⇒y=x2sinx+xcosx+x

Hence, yπ2=π24+π2.

q: Consider the statement "The match will be played only if the weather is good and ground is not wet". Select the correct negation from the following:
question: Consider the statement "The match will be played only if the weather is good and ground is not wet". Select the correct negation from the following:
options: The match will not be played and weather is not good and ground is wet.
If the match will not be played, then either weather is not good or ground is wet.
The match will be played and weather is not good or ground is wet.
The match will not be played or weather is good and ground is not wet.
solution:
Let p: weather is good
and q: ground is not wet

Given statement "The match will be played only if the weather is good and ground is not wet" can be written as p∧q

Now, negation of  p∧q is ~p∧q≡~p∨~q

which is equivalent to "The match will be played and weather is not good or ground is wet".

solution:
When a coin is tossed, then probability of head, PHead=PTail=12

Therefore, 1-P (All tail)≥0.9

⇒1-12n≥0.9

⇒12n≤110

solution:
We have, x2-x-12=0

⇒x2-4x+3x-12=0

⇒x+3x-4=0

⇒x=4 as x≠-3

⇒x=±2

Hence, number of real solution is 2.

q: The first of the two samples in a group has <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn></math> items with mean <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn></math> and standard deviation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>.</mo></math> If the whole group has <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>250</mn></math> items with mean <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>6</mn></math> and standard deviation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mn>13</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>44</mn></msqrt><mo>,</mo></math> then the standard deviation of the second sample is:

solution:
We have, number of terms in the first group n1=100 with their mean X1=15 and number of terms in whole group is n1+n2=250 and their mean X=15.6.

Variance of first groupV1x=9 and whole group is Varx=13.44.

Now, σ2=n1σ12+n2σ22n1+n2+n1n2n1+n22x¯1-x¯22

and n2=150,x¯2=16,V2(x)=σ2

Therefore, 13.44=100×9+150×σ22250+100×1502502×1

⇒σ22=16

⇒σ2=4
